title: Border Patrol Agents Kill ICU Nurse Alex Pretti in Minneapolis
excerpt: Border Patrol agents shot and killed Alex Pretti, a 37-year-old ICU nurse and U.S. citizen, during an immigration enforcement operation in Minneapolis — the second fatal shooting by federal agents in the city in under three weeks. Video evidence and eyewitness accounts contradicted the government's initial account that Pretti was brandishing a firearm.
content:
On January 24, 2026, U.S. Border Patrol agents shot and killed Alex Jeffrey Pretti, a 37-year-old ICU nurse at the Minneapolis VA hospital, near the intersection of 26th Street and Nicollet Avenue in Minneapolis. Pretti had no criminal record and held a valid Minnesota permit to carry the handgun he was licensed to own.

  • DHS initially stated Pretti had "approached US Border Patrol officers with a 9 mm semi-automatic handgun" and that an agent fired defensive shots after Pretti "violently resisted" disarmament.
  • Multiple bystander videos — verified by The Wall Street Journal, The New York Times, The Washington Post, and CNN — showed Pretti filming the scene on his phone and checking on a woman pushed by agents before being tackled and shot, with no footage showing him brandishing a weapon.
  • A physician who witnessed the shooting testified in a sworn court affidavit that federal agents initially blocked them from administering first aid, and that agents appeared to be counting bullet wounds rather than performing CPR.
  • CNN analysis found the two agents at the center of the incident escalated the confrontation from the start, and experts noted that Border Patrol and CBP officers are not typically trained for roving patrols in urban protest environments.
  • The killing was the second fatal shooting by federal immigration agents in Minneapolis in less than three weeks, following the January 7 killing of Renee Good. Border Patrol Commander-at-Large Gregory Bovino was stripped of his title days after the shooting.
  • Minnesota state authorities attempted to access the shooting scene but were physically blocked by federal officers. The state sued the Trump administration, accusing it of withholding evidence.
"Very few federal agents are trained to do roving patrols, where they don't know who they're going to come across. That's more of a state and local police function," said a law enforcement expert quoted by CNN.
mediaPlaceholder: Bystander video of the moments before and during Pretti's shooting, showing him holding a phone and checking on another person
imageAlt: Border Patrol Agents Kill ICU Nurse Alex Pretti in Minneapolis
